message:
feat: add self-update command for seamless upgrades

Enables users to update NTM to the latest version directly from the CLI
without manual download or package manager commands.

**New Package: internal/updater/**

Core functionality:
- GitHub API integration for release discovery
- Semantic version comparison with pre-release handling
- Platform detection (OS + architecture)
- Binary download with progress indication
- Atomic replacement with backup of existing binary
- Checksum verification when available

Safety features:
- Creates backup before replacing binary
- Validates downloaded file before installation
- Handles both tar.gz archives and raw binaries
- Cleans up temporary files on success or failure

**New Command: ntm upgrade (internal/cli/upgrade.go)**

Usage:
  ntm upgrade           # Check for updates and prompt to install
  ntm upgrade --check   # Check only, don't install
  ntm upgrade --yes     # Auto-confirm installation
  ntm upgrade --force   # Force reinstall even if up-to-date

Features:
- Compares current version against latest GitHub release
- Shows release notes and changelog
- Styled output with theme colors
- Progress indication during download
- Success/error messaging with next steps

Version comparison:
- Handles semantic versions (v1.2.3)
- Supports pre-release tags (v1.2.3-beta.1)
- Skips pre-releases unless explicitly requested

Platform support:
- Automatically detects darwin/linux and amd64/arm64
- Downloads appropriate binary for current platform
- Handles architecture naming variations

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -41,7 +41,7 @@
 {"id":"ntm-8h3.1","title":"File Context Injection (--file flag)","description":"Add --file flag to ntm send to inject file contents into prompts.

## Use Case
Instead of manually copying file contents, specify files to include:
```bash
ntm send myproject --all \
  --file=src/auth.py \
  --file=src/models.py \
  "Refactor these to use dependency injection"
```

## Prompt Injection Format
Files are prepended to prompt with clear formatting:
```
# File: src/auth.py
\`\`\`python
<contents of src/auth.py>
\`\`\`

# File: src/models.py
\`\`\`python
<contents of src/models.py>
\`\`\`

---

Refactor these to use dependency injection
```

## Extended Syntax
```bash
--file=path                    # Entire file
--file=path:10-50              # Lines 10-50
--file=path:10-                # Line 10 to end
--file=path:-50                # First 50 lines
--file=path:func:my_function   # Extract function (future)
```

## Implementation
```go
type FileSpec struct {
    Path      string
    StartLine int  // 0 = from beginning
    EndLine   int  // 0 = to end
}

func ParseFileSpec(spec string) (FileSpec, error) {
    // Parse "path:start-end" format
}

func InjectFiles(specs []FileSpec, prompt string) (string, error) {
    var parts []string
    
    for _, spec := range specs {
        content, err := readFileRange(spec)
        if err != nil {
            return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to read %s: %w", spec.Path, err)
        }
        
        lang := detectLanguage(spec.Path)
        header := fmt.Sprintf("# File: %s", spec.Path)
        if spec.StartLine > 0 || spec.EndLine > 0 {
            header += fmt.Sprintf(" (lines %d-%d)", spec.StartLine, spec.EndLine)
        }
        
        parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("%s\n```%s\n%s\n```", 
            header, lang, content))
    }
    
    parts = append(parts, "---\n\n"+prompt)
    return strings.Join(parts, "\n\n"), nil
}
```

## Language Detection
Map file extensions to markdown language tags:
```go
var langMap = map[string]string{
    ".py":   "python",
    ".go":   "go",
    ".js":   "javascript",
    ".ts":   "typescript",
    ".rs":   "rust",
    ".rb":   "ruby",
    ".java": "java",
    // etc.
}
```

## Integration with Templates
Templates can reference {{file}} and {{files}}:
```markdown
---
name: review
variables:
  file: required
---
Review the following file for issues:

{{file}}

Focus on security and performance.
```

## Flags
| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| -f, --file | File to include (repeatable) |
| --file-format | Format: markdown (default), raw, xml |
| --no-file-header | Omit file path headers |

## Edge Cases
- File not found: Error with clear message
- Binary files: Skip with warning
- Very large files: Warn if > 50KB, --force to override
- Relative vs absolute paths: Resolve relative to cwd

## Files to Modify
- internal/cli/send.go - Add --file flag processing
- internal/prompt/inject.go - File injection logic (new)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:43:58.519465-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:43:58.519465-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-8h3.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-8h3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:43:58.520574-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

-{"id":"ntm-a8q","title":"Implement --robot-ack for send confirmation","description":"Track and confirm message receipt by agents.

## Rationale
After --robot-send, I need to know:
1. Did the agent receive the message?
2. Did the agent acknowledge understanding?
3. Is the agent actively working on it?

Without this, I'm blind after sending - I have to poll tail output and parse for confirmation patterns.

## Proposal: Ack Tracking
When sending with --robot-send --track, ntm:
1. Watches pane output for acknowledgment patterns
2. Returns structured confirmation

## Output Structure
```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "sent_at": "ISO8601",
  "confirmations": [
    {
      "pane": "0.1",
      "ack_type": "prompt_returned",  // agent showed ready prompt
      "ack_at": "ISO8601",
      "latency_ms": 234
    },
    {
      "pane": "0.2",
      "ack_type": "echo_detected",    // agent echoed input
      "ack_at": "ISO8601",
      "latency_ms": 156
    }
  ],
  "pending": ["0.3"],  // still waiting
  "failed": []
}
```

## Ack Detection Patterns
- prompt_returned: Agent shows ready prompt after processing
- echo_detected: Input echoed in pane (basic confirmation)
- explicit_ack: Agent responds with understood/acknowledged
- output_started: Agent begins producing output

## Timeout Handling
--timeout=30s - wait up to 30s for acks
Returns partial result with pending list if timeout","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:58:03.223434-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:58:03.223434-05:00"}

 {"id":"ntm-bcr","title":"EPIC: Session Presets (Recipes)","description":"","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:39:13.104435-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:39:13.104435-05:00","comments":[{"id":6,"issue_id":"ntm-bcr","author":"jemanuel","text":"-","created_at":"2025-12-10T07:42:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-bh5","title":"EPIC: Session Persistence & Recovery","description":"Sessions survive reboots and can be recreated from saved state.

## Problem Statement
Tmux sessions are ephemeral - a reboot or accidental kill loses everything:
- Agent counts, types, and arrangements must be manually recreated
- Working directory and project context is lost
- No way to 'bookmark' a useful session configuration
- Users avoid rebooting to preserve valuable sessions

## Goals
1. Capture complete session state (agents, layout, cwd, env)
2. Enable session recreation from saved state
3. Support periodic auto-snapshots for safety
4. Make session configs portable across machines

## Success Criteria
- `ntm session save` captures full session state to disk
- `ntm session restore` recreates session exactly
- Auto-snapshot on session creation (optional)
- `ntm session list --saved` shows available saved sessions

## Technical Approach
- Session state stored as JSON in ~/.local/share/ntm/sessions/
- Capture: session name, cwd, agent types/counts, layout, pane titles
- Restore: create session, spawn agents, apply layout
- Handle conflicts (session already exists) gracefully

## State Schema
```json
{
  "name": "myproject",
  "cwd": "/path/to/project",
  "agents": {"cc": 2, "cod": 1, "gmi": 1},
  "layout": "tiled",
  "panes": [{"title": "myproject__cc_1", "command": "claude"}],
  "created": "2025-12-10T10:00:00Z",
  "saved": "2025-12-10T12:00:00Z"
}
```

## User Value
- Fearless reboots - sessions are recoverable
- Consistent project setups across machines
- Reduced friction starting work each day
- Backup for accidental session kills","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:37:01.748915-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:37:01.748915-05:00"}
 {"id":"ntm-bh5.1","title":"Session State Capture","description":"Capture complete session state to JSON for later restoration.

## State to Capture
```go
type SessionState struct {
    // Identity
    Name      string    `json:"name"`
    SavedAt   time.Time `json:"saved_at"`
    
    // Context
    WorkDir   string    `json:"cwd"`
    GitBranch string    `json:"git_branch,omitempty"`
    GitRemote string    `json:"git_remote,omitempty"`
    
    // Agent Configuration
    Agents    AgentSpec `json:"agents"`  // {cc: 2, cod: 1, gmi: 1}
    
    // Pane Details (for exact recreation)
    Panes     []PaneState `json:"panes"`
    
    // Layout
    Layout    string    `json:"layout"`  // "tiled", "even-horizontal", etc.
    
    // Metadata
    CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`  // Original session creation
    Version   int       `json:"version"`     // Schema version for migrations
}

type PaneState struct {
    Title     string `json:"title"`      // e.g., "myproject__cc_1"
    Index     int    `json:"index"`
    AgentType string `json:"agent_type"` // "cc", "cod", "gmi", "user"
    Command   string `json:"command"`    // The agent launch command
    Active    bool   `json:"active"`     // Was this the active pane?
}
```

## Capture Process
1. Get session info from tmux
2. Get all panes with metadata
3. Detect working directory (from pane 0 or explicit)
4. Get git context if in repo
5. Serialize to JSON
6. Write to storage location

## Command Interface
```bash
ntm session save                 # Save current session (in tmux)
ntm session save myproject       # Save specific session
ntm session save --name=alias    # Save with custom name
ntm session save --overwrite     # Overwrite existing save
```

## Storage
Location: ~/.local/share/ntm/sessions/<name>.json

Directory structure:
```
~/.local/share/ntm/sessions/
├── myproject.json
├── frontend.json
└── backend.json
```

## Implementation
```go
func CaptureSessionState(sessionName string) (*SessionState, error) {
    session, err := tmux.GetSession(sessionName)
    if err \!= nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    panes, err := tmux.GetPanes(sessionName)
    if err \!= nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    // Extract agent configuration from pane names
    agents := countAgents(panes)
    
    // Get working directory
    cwd := detectWorkDir(panes)
    
    // Get git info if applicable
    gitBranch, gitRemote := getGitInfo(cwd)
    
    return &SessionState{
        Name:      sessionName,
        SavedAt:   time.Now(),
        WorkDir:   cwd,
        GitBranch: gitBranch,
        GitRemote: gitRemote,
        Agents:    agents,
        Panes:     mapPaneStates(panes),
        Layout:    session.Layout,
        CreatedAt: session.Created,
        Version:   1,
    }, nil
}
```

## Files to Create
- internal/session/state.go - State data structures
- internal/session/capture.go - Capture logic
- internal/session/storage.go - File I/O","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:41:31.459939-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:41:31.459939-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-bh5.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-bh5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:41:31.46201-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
@@ -61,6 +61,7 @@
 {"id":"ntm-dvz","title":"Implement --robot-snapshot --since for delta updates","description":"Add delta/diff mode to snapshot that only returns changes since a timestamp.

## Rationale
In a polling loop, re-reading full state every time wastes tokens massively. If I check every 5 seconds and only 1 agent produced 2 lines of output, I don't need the full 500-token snapshot - I need a 50-token delta.

## Output Structure
```json
{
  "ts": "ISO8601",
  "since": "ISO8601",
  "changes": [
    {"type": "agent_output", "session": "s", "pane": "0.1", "lines_added": 12},
    {"type": "agent_state", "session": "s", "pane": "0.2", "from": "active", "to": "idle"},
    {"type": "bead_closed", "id": "ntm-xxx"},
    {"type": "bead_started", "id": "ntm-yyy", "agent": "0.1"},
    {"type": "mail_received", "to": "AgentName", "count": 1},
    {"type": "alert_new", "msg": "agent 0.3 stuck"},
    {"type": "alert_resolved", "msg": "agent 0.3 stuck"}
  ]
}
```

## Implementation Notes
- Server must maintain a ring buffer of recent state changes
- Changes are coalesced (10 output events become 1 with lines_added=10)
- Empty changes array = nothing happened = immediate return
- If since is too old, fall back to full snapshot with warning

## Efficiency
This enables efficient polling: check every few seconds, act only on changes. 95% of polls will return near-empty diffs.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:50:26.267831-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:50:26.267831-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-dvz","depends_on_id":"ntm-y44","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:54:26.595883-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-dvz","depends_on_id":"ntm-sim","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:54:39.687164-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-dvz","depends_on_id":"ntm-hat","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:54:39.743316-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-ed5","title":"EPIC: Enhanced UI/Theme System","description":"","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:39:20.284079-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:39:20.284079-05:00","comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"ntm-ed5","author":"jemanuel","text":"-","created_at":"2025-12-10T07:42:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-eqs","title":"Add --tag filtering to ntm send and list","description":"Filter operations by agent tags.

## What This Task Does

Add --tag flag to commands that operate on agents:
- ntm send: Send to agents matching tag
- ntm list: Show only sessions with matching agents
- ntm status: Filter panes by tag
- ntm kill: Kill only agents matching tag

## Interface

$ ntm send --tag=frontend 'Update the UI'
# Sends to all agents tagged 'frontend' across all sessions

$ ntm send myproject --tag=backend 'Run migrations'
# Sends to 'backend' agents in myproject only

$ ntm list --tag=frontend
# Shows sessions that have at least one 'frontend' agent

$ ntm status myproject --tag=backend
# Shows only backend-tagged panes

## Matching Logic

Simple OR matching: agent matches if it has ANY of the specified tags.

$ ntm send --tag=frontend,ui 'message'
# Matches agents with 'frontend' OR 'ui' tag

Future: AND matching with special syntax
$ ntm send --tag='frontend+urgent' 'message'
# Matches agents with 'frontend' AND 'urgent'

## Implementation

1. Add --tag flag to send, list, status, kill commands
2. Parse tags from flag (comma-separated)
3. Filter pane list by tag match
4. Include tag info in JSON output

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] --tag works on ntm send
- [ ] --tag works on ntm list
- [ ] --tag works on ntm status  
- [ ] --tag works on ntm kill
- [ ] OR matching for multiple tags
- [ ] Tags included in JSON output

+{"id":"ntm-fpb","title":"Implement command history tracking","description":"Track and expose history of commands sent to agents.

## Rationale
For debugging, auditing, and understanding agent behavior, I need to know:
- What was sent to each agent and when?
- What was the response?
- How long did each command take?

This creates an audit trail and enables analysis.

## Storage
```go
type CommandEntry struct {
    ID        uint64
    Session   string
    Pane      string
    Command   string
    SentAt    time.Time
    AckAt     *time.Time       // when agent acked
    OutputLen int              // lines of output produced
    Duration  *time.Duration   // if completed
}
```

## Query Interface
```bash
ntm --robot-history myproject                    # all history
ntm --robot-history myproject --pane=0.1         # specific pane
ntm --robot-history myproject --last=10          # last N entries
ntm --robot-history myproject --since="1h ago"   # time-based
```

## Output Structure
```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "entries": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "pane": "0.1",
      "command": "implement feature X",
      "sent_at": "ISO8601",
      "ack_at": "ISO8601",
      "output_lines": 150,
      "duration_sec": 45
    }
  ],
  "total": 25
}
```

## Persistence
Store in SQLite DB per session:
~/.local/share/ntm/history/{session}.db

## Privacy Consideration
Commands may contain sensitive info. Consider:
- Optional encryption
- Configurable retention period
- Explicit --no-history flag","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:58:36.580871-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:58:36.580871-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-fpb","depends_on_id":"ntm-y44","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:59:11.256481-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-gcv","title":"Implement notification system with desktop and webhook support","description":"Create a notification system that can alert users via multiple channels.

## What This Task Does

Build infrastructure to send notifications when events occur:
- Desktop notifications (native OS)
- Webhook POST (Slack, Discord, custom)
- Shell command execution
- Log file appending

## Notification Events

- agent.error - Agent hit error state
- agent.crashed - Agent process exited
- agent.restarted - Agent was auto-restarted
- agent.idle - Agent waiting for input
- session.created - New session spawned
- session.killed - Session terminated
- health.degraded - Overall health dropped

## Configuration

[notifications]
enabled = true
events = ["agent.error", "agent.crashed", "agent.restarted"]

[notifications.desktop]
enabled = true

[notifications.webhook]
url = "https://hooks.slack.com/services/XXX/YYY/ZZZ"
# Payload template (Go template syntax)
template = '''
{"text": "NTM: {{.Event}} - {{.Message}}"}
'''

[notifications.shell]
command = "~/.config/ntm/notify.sh"
pass_json = true

[notifications.log]
path = "~/.local/share/ntm/notifications.jsonl"

## Implementation

Create internal/notify package:

type Event struct {
    Type      string
    Session   string
    Agent     string
    Message   string
    Timestamp time.Time
    Data      map[string]interface{}
}

type Notifier interface {
    Notify(event Event) error
}

Implementations:
- DesktopNotifier: osascript (macOS), notify-send (Linux)
- WebhookNotifier: HTTP POST with template rendering
- ShellNotifier: exec.Command with JSON stdin
- LogNotifier: Append to JSONL file

## Desktop Notifications

macOS:
osascript -e 'display notification "message" with title "NTM"'

Linux (notify-send):
notify-send "NTM" "message"

Windows/WSL:
powershell.exe -Command "[Windows.UI.Notifications...]"

## ntm notify command

Manual notification for testing/scripting:

$ ntm notify "Build complete" --session myproject
$ ntm notify --event agent.error --message "Rate limit hit"

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Desktop notifications work on macOS and Linux
- [ ] Webhook POST sends correctly formatted payload
- [ ] Shell command receives event as JSON
- [ ] Events can be filtered in config
- [ ] ntm notify command for manual triggers
- [ ] Errors in notification don't break NTM

 {"id":"ntm-gk5","title":"Integrate agent-mail into robot snapshot","description":"Include agent-mail status in unified snapshot.

## Rationale
Agent-mail is how agents coordinate. The snapshot needs to include mail status so orchestrator knows about pending communications.

## Integration Points
1. Check if agent-mail project exists for current working dir
2. Query agent-mail for registered agents
3. Map tmux panes to agent-mail identities
4. Include unread counts and pending ack counts

## Snapshot Extension
```json
{
  "...": "existing fields",
  "agent_mail": {
    "project": "/path/to/project",
    "agents": {
      "BlueLake": {"pane": "0.1", "unread": 2, "pending_ack": 1},
      "GreenCastle": {"pane": "0.2", "unread": 0, "pending_ack": 0}
    },
    "total_unread": 2,
    "threads_active": 3
  }
}
```

## Challenges
- Agent names in mail != pane identifiers (need mapping)
- Agent-mail may not be installed/configured
- Multiple projects may be active

## Graceful Degradation
If agent-mail not available, include:
```json
{"agent_mail": {"available": false, "reason": "not configured"}}

 {"id":"ntm-k7o","title":"Integrate --ssh flag with all session commands","description":"Add --ssh support to spawn, list, attach, send, status, kill.

## What This Task Does

After the SSH wrapper (ntm-5zs) is complete, integrate --ssh flag
with all commands that operate on sessions.

## Commands to Update

- ntm spawn --ssh host: Create session on remote
- ntm list --ssh host: List remote sessions
- ntm attach --ssh host: Attach to remote session
- ntm send --ssh host: Send prompt to remote agents
- ntm status --ssh host: Get remote session status
- ntm kill --ssh host: Kill remote session

## Implementation Pattern

For each command:
1. Add --ssh flag (string, optional)
2. Pass to TmuxClient constructor
3. All tmux operations use client abstraction

func runSpawn(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    sshHost, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("ssh")
    client := tmux.NewClient(sshHost)
    
    // All operations use client
    if err := client.CreateSession(session); err != nil {
        return err
    }
    ...
}

## Session Host Persistence

When spawning remotely, store host in tmux environment:
client.SetEnvironment(session, "NTM_REMOTE_HOST", sshHost)

When operating on existing session without --ssh:
1. Get session's NTM_REMOTE_HOST
2. If set, use it
3. If not, assume local

## Error Handling

- Connection timeout: Clear message, suggest checking connectivity
- Auth failure: Suggest ssh-agent or key path
- Remote tmux not found: Suggest installing tmux on remote

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] --ssh works on spawn
- [ ] --ssh works on list  
- [ ] --ssh works on attach
- [ ] --ssh works on send
- [ ] --ssh works on status
- [ ] --ssh works on kill
- [ ] Remote host auto-detected for existing sessions

{"beads": {"available": false, "reason": "no .beads/ directory"}}

 {"id":"ntm-laa","title":"EPIC: Resilience & Reliability","description":"Make NTM self-healing: detect failures, recover automatically, and notify users.

## Problem Statement

When running multiple AI agents over extended periods, failures are inevitable:

1. **Agent Crashes**: Agents can crash, exit unexpectedly, or hang
2. **Rate Limits**: API rate limits cause temporary failures (429 errors)
3. **Silent Failures**: Users don't notice problems until manually checking
4. **Manual Recovery**: Every failure requires human intervention to restart
5. **Lost Work**: Crashed agents lose context and must start fresh

Currently, NTM provides no visibility into agent health and no recovery mechanisms.
Users must constantly monitor tmux panes to catch problems.

## Goals

1. Detect agent health issues (crashes, rate limits, errors, hangs)
2. Automatically restart crashed agents with configurable policies
3. Implement smart backoff for rate-limited agents
4. Push notifications when events occur (desktop, webhook, Slack)
5. Provide ntm health command for manual health checks

## Why This Matters

Reliability is the difference between a toy and a tool. Users need to trust that:
- If they start 4 agents at night, they'll still be running in the morning
- Problems are detected and reported, not silently ignored
- Recovery happens automatically when possible
- They're notified when intervention is truly needed

This transforms NTM from 'launch and babysit' to 'launch and trust'.

## Success Criteria

- ntm health shows status of all agents with clear indicators
- --auto-restart flag enables automatic crash recovery
- Rate limit detection triggers backoff, not crash
- Notifications fire on configurable events
- Agents survive transient failures without user intervention

## Technical Approach

Health detection via tmux output analysis:
- Process status: tmux list-panes shows if command is running
- Activity monitoring: Compare pane content over time
- Error patterns: Regex match for known error signatures
- Idle detection: Prompt pattern visible = waiting for input

Recovery mechanisms:
- Restart: Re-run agent command in same pane
- Backoff: Exponential delay on rate limit detection
- Limits: Max restart count to prevent infinite loops
- Alerting: Notify after N failures

## Notification Channels

- Desktop: osascript (macOS), notify-send (Linux)
- Webhook: HTTP POST to configurable URL (Slack, Discord, custom)
- Shell: Run custom command with event data
- Log: Append to file for later review

## Relation to ntm-4pp (Observability)

ntm-4pp focuses on OBSERVABILITY: seeing what agents are doing.
This epic focuses on RELIABILITY: keeping agents running.

They are complementary:
- ntm-4pp: 'Agent 3 hit a rate limit' (observation)
- This epic: 'Agent 3 hit a rate limit, backing off 60s, will retry' (action)

## Configuration

[resilience]
auto_restart = true
max_restarts = 3
restart_delay_seconds = 30
rate_limit_backoff = true
backoff_multiplier = 2.0
max_backoff_seconds = 300

[notifications]
enabled = true
events = ["agent.error", "agent.restarted", "session.unhealthy"]

[notifications.desktop]
enabled = true

[notifications.webhook]
url = "https://hooks.slack.com/..."

+{"id":"ntm-lla","title":"Implement session state save/restore","description":"Save and restore session state for disaster recovery.

## Rationale
If I'm orchestrating 8 agents on a complex task and:
- System crashes
- Network disconnects
- Need to pause and resume later

I need to be able to save the current state and restore it.

## Save Operation
```bash
ntm --robot-save myproject --output=state.json
ntm --robot-save myproject  # auto-saves to ~/.local/share/ntm/saves/
```

## State Includes
```json
{
  "saved_at": "ISO8601",
  "session": "myproject",
  "working_dir": "/path/to/project",
  "agents": [
    {
      "pane": "0.1",
      "type": "claude",
      "command": "claude",
      "recent_context": ["last 50 lines..."]
    }
  ],
  "layout": "tiled",
  "active_pane": "0.1",
  "command_history": [...],
  "beads_state": {...}  // if beads integration available
}
```

## Restore Operation
```bash
ntm --robot-restore state.json
ntm --robot-restore state.json --dry-run  # show what would happen
```

## Restore Process
1. Create session with same name
2. Create panes with correct layout
3. Start agents in each pane
4. Optionally replay context (send last N lines as context)

## Limitations
- Cannot restore exact agent state (no agent-side state)
- Context replay is best-effort
- External state (files changed) not tracked","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:59:00.354106-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:59:00.354106-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-lla","depends_on_id":"ntm-y44","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:59:11.312666-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-oba","title":"Implement --robot-terse for ultra-compact output","description":"Single-line encoded state for extremely token-constrained scenarios.

## Rationale
Sometimes I need just the essentials in minimum tokens. A full snapshot might be 500+ tokens; terse mode gives me the critical state in <50 tokens.

## Usage
```bash
ntm --robot-terse
ntm --robot-snapshot --terse  # terse snapshot
```

## Output Format
```
S:myproject|A:3/3|R:30|B:27|I:3|M:5|!:2
```

Decoded:
- S: Session name
- A: Agents (active/total)
- R: Ready beads
- B: Blocked beads  
- I: In-progress beads
- M: Unread mail
- !: Alert count

## Multi-Session
```
S:proj1|A:2/2|R:15|B:10|I:1|M:2|!:0;S:proj2|A:3/3|R:20|B:5|I:2|M:0|!:1
```

## Use Cases
1. Polling in tight loops
2. Embedding in prompts with strict token limits
3. Quick health checks
4. Dashboard status bars

## Parsing
Provide parsing utilities in robot package:
```go
func ParseTerse(s string) *TerseState
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:52:39.009743-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:52:39.009743-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-oba","depends_on_id":"ntm-y44","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:54:27.089343-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-oba","depends_on_id":"ntm-sim","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:54:39.818091-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-rlp","title":"Implement alert generation and tracking","description":"Generate actionable alerts from state analysis.

## Rationale
Alerts surface problems proactively. Instead of polling and analyzing raw state, the orchestrator gets pre-computed alerts.

## Alert Types
1. **Agent Stuck**: No output for N minutes
2. **Agent Crashed**: Pane process exited
3. **High CPU**: Agent consuming excessive resources
4. **Disk Low**: Running low on disk space
5. **Bead Stale**: In-progress bead with no activity
6. **Mail Backlog**: Many unread messages
7. **Dependency Cycle**: Bead dependency loop detected

## Alert Structure
```go
type Alert struct {
    ID        string    // unique alert ID
    Type      string    // agent_stuck, disk_low, etc.
    Severity  string    // info, warning, error, critical
    Message   string    // human-readable description
    Context   map[string]interface{} // relevant data
    CreatedAt time.Time
    ResolvedAt *time.Time
}
```

## Alert Lifecycle
1. Condition detected → Alert created
2. Alert included in snapshot/health output
3. Condition resolved → Alert marked resolved
4. Resolved alerts pruned after configurable time

## Configurable Thresholds
Via config file:
```toml
[alerts]
agent_stuck_minutes = 5
disk_low_threshold_gb = 5
mail_backlog_threshold = 10
```","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:54:13.05345-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:54:13.05345-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-rlp","depends_on_id":"ntm-y44","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:54:27.512569-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-rm4","title":"Add --json support to ntm status command","description":"Implement JSON output for the status command.

## What This Task Does

Modify the ntm status command to support --json flag for detailed session state.

## JSON Schema

{
  "session": "myproject",
  "exists": true,
  "attached": false,
  "window_count": 1,
  "panes": [
    {
      "index": 0,
      "title": "myproject__cc_1",
      "agent_type": "claude",
      "agent_number": 1,
      "dimensions": {"width": 80, "height": 24},
      "pid": 12345,
      "command": "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions",
      "active": true
    }
  ],
  "agents": {
    "claude": 2,
    "codex": 1,
    "gemini": 0
  },
  "working_directory": "/path/to/project",
  "created_at": "2025-12-10T10:00:00Z"
}

## Implementation

1. Import internal/output package
2. Create SessionStatusOutput and PaneInfo structs
3. Modify runStatus to build structured data
4. Include all pane details in JSON
5. Parse agent type from pane titles

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ntm status myproject --json produces valid JSON
- [ ] All pane information is included
- [ ] Agent type parsed from title
- [ ] Non-existent session returns error JSON
- [ ] Text output unchanged

-{"id":"ntm-sc6","title":"Implement agent progress detection","description":"Detect and report agent progress through work items.

## Rationale
When an agent is working on a task, I need to understand progress:
- Is it just starting?
- Is it midway through?
- Is it finishing up?
- Has it encountered a blocker?

This enables smarter orchestration (don't interrupt agents near completion, reassign stuck work, etc.)

## Progress Indicators
Detect progress from pane output patterns:

### Starting Indicators
- 'Let me', 'I'll', 'Starting'
- File reads, searches
- Planning/analysis output

### Working Indicators
- File edits
- Code blocks being written
- Test runs
- Build commands

### Finishing Indicators
- 'Done', 'Complete', 'Finished'
- Summary output
- Return to prompt
- Commit messages

### Stuck Indicators
- 'Error', 'Failed', 'Cannot'
- Repeated attempts
- Questions asked
- Long pauses

## Output in Snapshot
```json
{
  "agents": [{
    "pane": "0.1",
    "progress": {
      "stage": "working",     // starting, working, finishing, stuck
      "confidence": 0.75,
      "indicators": ["file_edits", "code_blocks"],
      "time_in_stage_sec": 120
    }
  }]
}
```

## Future: ML-based Detection
Could train a classifier on agent outputs to detect progress more accurately. For now, heuristic-based is sufficient.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:58:19.308214-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:58:19.308214-05:00"}

 {"id":"ntm-sim","title":"Implement --robot-snapshot for unified state query","description":"Core orchestration primitive: single command returns complete system state including all sessions, agent health, bead summary, mail status, and alerts. This is THE most important flag because it eliminates the need for multiple queries.

## Rationale
When orchestrating N agents across M sessions working on P beads with Q mail threads, I currently need separate queries for each dimension. This compounds latency (4-6 tool calls) and burns tokens on redundant parsing. A unified snapshot gives me complete situational awareness in one atomic read.

## Output Structure
```json
{
  "ts": "ISO8601",
  "sessions": [{
    "name": "string",
    "agents": [{
      "pane": "window.pane",
      "type": "claude|codex|gemini|cursor|windsurf|aider",
      "state": "active|idle|stuck|waiting",
      "last_output_age_sec": number,
      "output_tail_lines": number,
      "current_bead": "bead-id|null",
      "pending_mail": number
    }]
  }],
  "beads_summary": {"open": N, "in_progress": N, "blocked": N, "ready": N},
  "mail_unread": number,
  "alerts": ["string"]
}
```

## Key Design Decisions
- Always include everything (no optional fields that require follow-up)
- Use compact field names (abbreviations OK for frequently-accessed data)
- Include computed fields (last_output_age_sec vs raw timestamp)
- Alerts are pre-filtered to actionable items only

## Integration Points
- tmux: session/pane enumeration, output capture
- beads: bd list --json integration  
- agent-mail: inbox count per agent
- pane title parsing for agent type detection","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:50:07.458116-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:50:07.458116-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-sim","depends_on_id":"ntm-y44","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:54:26.544338-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"ntm-sim","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Implementation Architecture

### File Structure
```
internal/robot/
├── robot.go          # Existing - add Snapshot type and function
├── snapshot.go       # NEW - SnapshotOutput, BuildSnapshot()
├── integrations.go   # NEW - BeadsIntegration, MailIntegration
└── alerts.go         # NEW - AlertManager, Alert types
```

### Core Type
```go
type SnapshotOutput struct {
    Timestamp   time.Time              `json:"ts"`
    Sessions    []SessionSnapshot      `json:"sessions"`
    Beads       *BeadsSnapshot         `json:"beads,omitempty"`
    AgentMail   *MailSnapshot          `json:"agent_mail,omitempty"`
    Alerts      []Alert                `json:"alerts"`
    Summary     SnapshotSummary        `json:"summary"`
}
```

### Flag Registration
In root.go init():
```go
rootCmd.Flags().Bool("robot-snapshot", false, "Output complete system state as JSON")
rootCmd.Flags().String("since", "", "Return only changes since timestamp (ISO8601)")
```

### Performance Target
- Cold snapshot: <100ms
- With caching: <20ms
- Memory: <5MB for 10 sessions with 50 panes","created_at":"2025-12-10T07:55:34Z"}]}
